title: Fabrication of Microneedle Patches
author: Stephania Konstantinidi
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8cehste
materials: The microneedles are ready for use.

steps.title: Heat deionised water to 100 degrees...
steps.instruction: Heat deionised water to 100 degrees Celsius 100 °C using a becher.

steps.title: Put the mold(s) into the water and stir softly
steps.instruction: Put the mold(s) into the water and stir softly.

steps.title: Remove the molds and let them...
steps.instruction: Remove the molds and let them dry in a chemical hood for 1-2 hours.

steps.title: Stick the molds on the bottom...
steps.instruction: Stick the molds on the bottom of an adequate recipient using double-sided tape.

steps.title: Add 4g of poly(vinyl alcohol) (30−...
steps.instruction: Add 4g of poly(vinyl alcohol) (30− 70 kDa, 10 wt %) powder and stir until transparent.

steps.title: Heat 28g of deionised water to...
steps.instruction: Heat 28g of deionised water to 110 degrees Celsius 110 °C .

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 mL of PVA solution on top of the mold.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 rmp for 20...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 rmp for 20 minutes at 40 degrees Celsius.

steps.title: Let the microneedles dry under a chemical hood overnight
steps.instruction: Let the microneedles dry under a chemical hood overnight.

steps.title: Remove the microneedles from the molds
steps.instruction: Remove the microneedles from the molds.
